title: Chouchou Refuses to Abandon the Shop
description: On pages 8–11, Chouchou’s loyalty to the pet food shop turns Buggy’s occupation from a pirate threat into a personal wound for the town.
scene: Chouchou refuses to give up the place connected to his owner, while pirates and bystanders react to the dog’s stubborn defense.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Don't you pick on poor ol' chou- chou!

dialogue.speaker: Mayor Boodle
dialogue.text: I'm the mayor of this town...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: He's resting. My house is just over there.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: THAT BOY'S LOST A LOT OF BLOOD!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I told 'Im there's a doctor at the refugee shelter, but he said he just needs a little sleep!!

dialogue.speaker: Mayor Boodle
dialogue.text: WHY'S HE THE ONLY ONE LEFT IN TOWN?

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: OH! SO HE'S A GUARD DOG!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: A good friend of mine owned this store.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: ...he and chouchou opened this little shop.

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: They've got a lot of memories here

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: CHOUCHOU! You're in charge while I'm gone.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: DON'T EAT UP ALL THE MERCHAN- DISE, Y'HEAR!?

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: I'm sure this shop means a lot to his master, but this is just cruel.

dialogue.speaker: Chouchou
dialogue.text: Is he at the refugee shelter with the others?

summary: This part makes the conflict emotional: Luffy sees that Buggy’s violence is not abstract, but aimed at things people love and protect.
